verticals.title: Startup & SaaS Funding (2026): Real Options When Banks Say “Come Back Later”
verticals.description: A brutally honest funding map for startups and SaaS founders—what’s realistic, what’s hype, and what actually gets approved.

hero.h1: Startup & SaaS Funding Without the Fairy Tales
hero.subhead: If your bank wants 2+ years of profitability, congrats: you’ve met capitalism. Here are the options that actually exist.

seo.metaTitle: Startup & SaaS Funding (2026) — Real Options That Actually Work
seo.metaDescription: Explore funding paths for startups and SaaS: revenue-based financing, lines of credit, business credit, founders’ strategy, and what lenders really want.

options.title: Revenue-Based Financing (RBF)
options.desc: If you have consistent revenue, this can beat begging for a bank loan.

options.title: Business Credit + 0% Intro (Credit Stacking)
options.desc: Works when your personal credit is strong and your story is clean.

options.title: Line of Credit (Cash Flow Buffer)
options.desc: Best used to smooth cash flow—not to fund a dream that doesn’t sell.

choices.label: I have consistent revenue (MRR/ARR) already
choices.result: RBF or LOC are your best starting points.

choices.label: I’m pre-revenue / launching
choices.result: Business credit strategy + lean launch plan. Avoid predatory debt.

choices.label: I’m scaling and need marketing/inventory/cash buffer
choices.result: RBF/LOC + cash flow discipline. Get a plan, not a prayer.

faqs.q: Can a startup get a traditional bank loan?
faqs.a: Sometimes, but banks usually want strong time-in-business, cash flow, and collateral. Most early-stage founders do better with alternative paths.

faqs.q: Is revenue-based financing a good idea?
faqs.a: It can be—if your revenue is consistent and you understand the payback mechanics. It’s not “free money,” it’s structured cash flow.
If you have strong deposits and clean banking history, you’ll typically move faster than pre-revenue founders.

compliance.disclaimerShort: Funding options vary by profile. Not a lender. Terms depend on underwriting.

relatedGuides.title: RBF vs Line of Credit: What’s the difference?

internalLinkAnchors: line of credit for saas founders
